title: Supernova Light Curve Fitting
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Supernova Light Curve Fitting: Type Ia supernova light curve fitting: extract distance modulus and nuisance parameters from multi-band photometry. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.sed.supernova_template…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; host_galaxy_dust_uncertainty, peculiar_velocity_km_s set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: m_x = M_x + mu + alpha*x1 - beta*c + delta_M*step(M_stellar); SALT2/SNooPy template
E.world_state_x: distance modulus mu = 5*log10(d_L/10pc), stretch x1, color c
E.observation_y: multi-band photometry {m_B, m_V, m_R} vs. time for N_epochs epochs

W.regime: Existence of the recovered lc_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); host_galaxy_dust_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Observation gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: sigma(mu) < 0.15 mag per SN for well-sampled LC. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |host_galaxy_dust_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_epochs * N_bands * N_params) for template chi2 per iteration
